exporter_welcome: This tool exports your mod setup from Windows so you can import it on Linux.
source_select_desc: Select which mod manager(s) to export from.
source_mo2_desc: Export mods managed by Mod Organizer 2.
source_both_desc: Export from both mod managers at once.
scan_none_found_desc: No MO2 or Vortex installations were found on this computer. Make sure the mod manager is installed.
game_select_desc: Choose which games to export. Deselect games you don't need.
game_duplicate_warning: Some games appear in both MO2 and Vortex. You may want to deselect duplicates to avoid conflicts.
export_options_desc: Choose how to export your mods.
export_mode_metadata: Metadata only (small file, re-download mods on Linux)
export_mode_network: Network transfer (send directly to Linux PC)
export_mode_metadata_desc: Creates a small .gbw file (~KB) with load order, Nexus IDs, and categories. You'll need to re-download mod files on Linux.
export_mode_full_desc: Copies all mod files + metadata to a target folder (USB drive, external HDD). Can be very large.
export_mode_network_desc: Starts a local server. Your Linux PC connects over LAN and pulls the data directly.
export_warning_no_nexus: {count} of {total} mods have no Nexus ID — these cannot be re-downloaded automatically.
target_desc: Choose where to save the exported data.
target_folder_desc: Save to a folder on a USB stick, external hard drive, or internal drive.
network_instructions: On your Linux PC, open HelloLinux and enter this IP and PIN.
network_vortex_unsupported: Network transfer is not yet supported for Vortex data. Please use folder export instead.
done_step1_metadata: 1. Copy the .gbw file to your Linux PC (USB, cloud, email...)
done_step1_full: 1. Connect the drive to your Linux PC
done_step2: 2. Open HelloLinux on your Linux PC
done_step3: 3. Select the file/folder and choose your target mod manager
importer_welcome: Import your Windows mod setup into a Linux mod manager.
source_title: Where are your mods coming from?
source_file_desc: Select a .gbw file created by the Windows exporter.
source_folder_desc: Select the folder where the full export was saved.
source_ntfs_desc: Scan mounted Windows partitions for MO2 instances.
source_network_desc: Connect to a Windows PC running the GoodbyeWindows Exporter.
network_enter_ip: Enter the IP address shown on your Windows PC:
target_anvil_desc: Import into Anvil Organizer's instance format.
target_amethyst_desc: Import into Amethyst Mod Manager's staging format.
import_mods_download: {count} mods need to be downloaded from Nexus
import_mods_missing: {count} mods without Nexus ID (manual download needed)
import_done_summary: {imported} mods imported, {missing} mods need manual download.
import_done_nexus_links: Nexus links for missing mods have been copied to clipboard.
hl_preview_desc: These games were found in your source. Review before importing.
hl_needs_download: Mod files not included — mods will need to be re-downloaded on Linux.
hl_metadata_only: {count} mods without Nexus ID cannot be re-downloaded automatically.
hl_config_desc: Choose your target mod manager and set game paths.
hl_game_path_hint: Set the Linux game installation path for each game. You can leave this empty and configure it later in the mod manager.
hl_framework_hint: Framework mods (SKSE, Script Extender, etc.) will be detected and handled automatically by Anvil.
hl_framework_done_hint: Framework mods were imported normally. Anvil will detect and manage them automatically when you open the instance.
hl_ip_pin_required: IP address and PIN are required.
